top_stories.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
top_stories.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities in its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, including flaws that could allow arbitrary code execution and user impersonation. One of the CVEs (CVE-2026-20184, CVSS 9.8) involves improper certificate validation in SSO integration, directly impacting identity and authentication infrastructure.

top_stories.title: Vishing attacks on Okta identity systems on the rise
top_stories.summary: Vishing attacks targeting Okta identity systems are increasing, with attackers calling victims or IT help desks to socially engineer MFA resets or weakening. LevelBlue researchers published findings on April 13 detailing the attack methodology and what occurs once Okta is compromised through these voice-based social engineering techniques.

top_stories.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
top_stories.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service kit have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique abuses legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to bypass MFA, tricking victims into granting account access through a service's authentic new-device login process.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. The update includes two zero-day vulnerabilities among the elevation-of-privilege flaws, making it directly relevant to PAM and access control security.

top_stories.title: Microsoft's April patch puts Windows domain controllers into reboot loops — third known issue from KB5082063 is affecting Windows Server 2016 through 2025
top_stories.summary: Microsoft's April patch KB5082063 is causing continuous reboot loops on Windows Server domain controllers running versions 2016 through 2025. This is the third known issue associated with the update, directly affecting enterprise Active Directory infrastructure that underpins authentication and identity services.

news.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted focused on managing non-human identities (NHIs) such as service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants in enterprise environments. The content references 2024 statistics citing unmanaged non-human identities as a factor in 68% of cloud breaches, addressing lifecycle management of automated credentials.

news.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
news.summary: Three zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender—BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend—are being actively exploited by threat actors to gain elevated privileges on compromised systems. Two of the three flaws remain unpatched, with Huntress issuing warnings about ongoing exploitation activity.

news.title: Sam Altman's project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder. | TechCrunch
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project, which uses iris-scanning Orbs for anonymous human verification, is expanding through new partnerships including with Tinder. The initiative focuses on proving "proof of humanity" to distinguish humans from AI bots online.

news.title: Sam Altman's World Teams With Zoom, Tinder to Better Verify Humans in the AI Age
news.summary: Sam Altman's World (formerly Worldcoin) is expanding its iris-scanning human verification technology through partnerships with Tinder for U.S. user verification and Zoom's Deep Face feature. The integrations use World's biometric identity protocol to distinguish humans from AI in digital interactions.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World project launches major upgrade to fight deepfakes and bots
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has launched a major upgrade aimed at combating deepfakes and bots through its biometric identity verification system. The project uses iris-scanning technology to establish proof of human identity, positioning it as a digital identity authentication solution.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World releases major protocol upgrade, introduces World ID app
news.summary: Sam Altman's World has released a major protocol upgrade and launched the World ID app, a digital identity verification system. The product focuses on proving human authenticity in AI-driven environments using biometric-based identity credentials.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical-world security threats. It covers implementing 2FA as an additional security barrier against threat actors who bypass conventional security systems outside standard IT contexts.

news.title: SailPoint、AWSとの戦略的協業契約を締結　ユニファイド アイデンティティ ガバナンス レイヤーによるAIエージェントの保護を可能に
news.summary: SailPoint announced a strategic collaboration agreement with AWS to enable protection of AI agents through a unified identity governance layer. The partnership focuses on extending SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to secure agentic AI within AWS environments.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their AI agents (Copilot and Agentforce) that could have allowed external attackers to exfiltrate sensitive data. The flaws affected AI agent platforms rather than core IAM components, though they touch on authorization and data access controls within enterprise environments.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring MFA capabilities to web-based applications. No additional details about the product's specific features, target market, or technical implementation are available from the provided description.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops outlines five methods by which a Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on credential theft prevention and privilege escalation mitigation. The approach covers identity-first access controls, device trust enforcement, and lateral movement prevention.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a new backdoor designed to harvest credentials from major cloud environments including AWS, Google Azure, and Alibaba. The attack uses typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communication and targets cloud identity credentials specifically.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network infrastructure and detained its alleged developer, who created a toolkit used to steal account credentials and attempt over $20 million in fraud. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of detecting credential-based attacks, which increasingly blend in with normal user activity. It outlines detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt to identify compromised identity and authentication abuse.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites rather than using static page copies. The service effectively bypasses MFA protections by acting as a man-in-the-middle relay, making it resistant to standard anti-phishing takedown efforts.
